source.title: Prioritized memory access explains planning and hippocampal replay

confidence_rationale: Mattar & Daw provide the clearest computational framework for why hippocampal replay is ordered by utility -- specifically, by how much additional reward would be earned from better choices at each state. This 'prioritized memory access' framework maps closely onto ARC-018's viability map: the hippocampal representation is updated in proportion to its relevance to current decision-making under harm/goal error signals. The paper also proposes that dysfunction of this prioritisation mechanism underlies rumination and craving -- a striking parallel to the harm-valence component of ARC-018's viability map. Confidence slightly below peak because this is a theoretical/computational model, not electrophysiology, and the utility metric used (reward gain) is not identical to E3 harm/goal error.

mapping.source_claim_statement: A normative theory predicting which memories should be accessed at each moment to optimise future decisions: replay is ordered by utility (how much extra reward would be earned by better choices), balancing imminence of decision against value of propagating newly encountered information backwards.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-018 posits that the hippocampal viability map is updated by E3 harm/goal error. Mattar & Daw's utility metric is the closest biological-computational counterpart: it is the gradient of value with respect to state occupancy, which is what E3 harm/goal error represents when backpropagated through the viability map. The prioritisation account explains why the map would be selectively updated for states near current decisions -- exactly what ARC-018 requires for efficient planning.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The model uses reward gain (positive valence only) as the priority signal. ARC-018 includes harm as a first-class signal. The mapping assumes harm is encoded as negative utility -- an assumption supported by loss aversion findings but not directly tested in this paper.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical/computational model, validated against rodent replay data from spatial navigation tasks.

confidence_components.notes: Strong theoretical alignment. The risk is that real hippocampal replay may be less utility-optimal than the model predicts, and that harm signals may require separate treatment from reward signals.
